---
title: "Elevation"
description: "How high Canada is — an interactive relief map of the terrain, where the named peaks lie, and the distribution of land by elevation"
toc: false
resources:
  - vendor/**
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import os
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go

dist_csv = "data/geo/elevation_distribution.csv"
```

## The shape of the land

Seen whole, Canada's surface resolves into a few great physiographic regions: the high, folded
**Western Cordillera** along the Pacific; the flat-then-rising **Interior Plains** of the Prairies;
the ancient, low **Canadian Shield** wrapped around Hudson Bay; the **lowlands** of the St. Lawrence
and Hudson Bay; and the worn **Appalachian** uplands of the east. The map below is a **shaded-relief**
view of that terrain — pan and zoom to follow a valley or a coastline. Scattered across it are
Canada's **named summits**, each coloured by its elevation; hover for a name and height, and zoom in
to see the labels of the highest peak in each province and territory. The layer controls (top-right)
add optional context — cities, lakes and rivers, the **ecozones**, the **permafrost** zones, and the
**Arctic tree line** (the northern limit of the forest, where the taiga gives way to tundra).

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
import json
from pipeline.charts import MAJOR_CITIES


def _load(p):
    return json.load(open(p)) if os.path.exists(p) else {"type": "FeatureCollection", "features": []}


_data = {
    "peaks": _load("data/geo/peaks_canada.geojson"),
    "lakes": _load("data/geo/lakes_major.geojson"),
    "rivers": _load("data/geo/rivers_major.geojson"),
    "prov": _load("data/geo/prov_2021.geojson"),
    "ecozones": _load("data/geo/nef_ecozones.geojson"),
    "treeline": _load("data/geo/treeline.geojson"),
    "permafrost": _load("data/geo/permafrost_zones.geojson"),
    "cities": [{"name": c[0], "lat": c[1], "lon": c[2]} for c in MAJOR_CITIES],
}
print('<script>window.ELEV = ' + json.dumps(_data, separators=(",", ":")) + ';</script>')
```

::: {.column-screen-inset}

```{=html}
<style>
#elevmap{height:700px;border:1px solid #ddd;border-radius:6px;background:#e7ecef;}
.elev-legend{background:rgba(255,255,255,0.9);padding:7px 10px;border-radius:5px;font-size:12px;
  line-height:1.5;color:#333;box-shadow:0 1px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.2);}
.elev-legend i{display:inline-block;width:13px;height:13px;margin-right:6px;vertical-align:-2px;
  border:0.5px solid #888;}
.peak-label{background:transparent;border:0;box-shadow:none;color:#3a2a12;font-weight:600;
  font-size:11px;text-shadow:0 0 3px #fff,0 0 3px #fff,0 0 3px #fff;}
.city-label{background:transparent;border:0;box-shadow:none;color:#111;font-size:11px;
  text-shadow:0 0 3px #fff,0 0 3px #fff;}
.cat-legend{font-size:11px;line-height:1.5;max-width:330px;}
.cat-legend>div{column-gap:14px;}
.cat-legend i{width:11px;height:11px;}
.map-source{font-size:10px;color:#999;margin:6px 2px 0;}
</style>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="vendor/leaflet/leaflet.css"/>
<div id="elevmap"></div>
<div class="map-source">Relief: Natural Resources Canada — Canada Base Map · Elevation (CBME) hillshade,
served live (EPSG:3978). Peaks: Canadian Geographical Names (names &amp; coordinates) + MRDEM-30
(window-max elevation). Lakes &amp; rivers: NRCan Atlas. Ecozones &amp; tree line (taiga–tundra
boundary): AAFC/ECCC National Ecological Framework. Permafrost (~1995 mapping): Atlas of Canada
5th ed. (NRCan). All Open Government Licence – Canada.</div>
<!-- Quarto loads RequireJS for Plotly; Leaflet/proj4 are UMD and would register with AMD
     instead of creating globals (the same gotcha as window.Plotly). Hide `define` while the
     vendored libs load so they attach to window (L, proj4), then restore AMD for Plotly. -->
<script>window.__amdDefine=window.define;window.define=undefined;</script>
<script src="vendor/leaflet/leaflet.js"></script>
<script src="vendor/proj4.js"></script>
<script src="vendor/proj4leaflet.js"></script>
<script>window.define=window.__amdDefine;try{delete window.__amdDefine;}catch(e){}</script>
<script>
(function(){
  var E=window.ELEV; if(!E||typeof L==='undefined'){return;}
  // elevation → colour (green lowland → yellow → orange → red → purple high)
  var STOPS=[[0,'#1a9850'],[1000,'#a6d96a'],[2000,'#fee08b'],[3000,'#fdae61'],[4000,'#d73027'],[5500,'#7a0177']];
  function hx(s,i){return parseInt(s.substr(i,2),16);}
  function lerp(a,b,t){return 'rgb('+[1,3,5].map(function(i){return Math.round(hx(a,i)+(hx(b,i)-hx(a,i))*t);}).join(',')+')';}
  function elevColor(h){
    if(h<=STOPS[0][0])return STOPS[0][1];
    for(var i=1;i<STOPS.length;i++){if(h<=STOPS[i][0]){return lerp(STOPS[i-1][1],STOPS[i][1],(h-STOPS[i-1][0])/(STOPS[i][0]-STOPS[i-1][0]));}}
    return STOPS[STOPS.length-1][1];
  }
  // NRCan EPSG:3978 (Canada Atlas Lambert) tile matrix
  var res=[38364.660062653464,22489.62831258996,13229.193125052918,7937.5158750317505,
    4630.2175937685215,2645.8376250181442,1587.5031750063501,926.0435187537042,529.1675250036289,
    317.50063500127004,185.20870458407753,111.12522275044652,66.1501587502646,38.36466006265346,
    22.48962831258996,13.229193125052918,7.9375158750317505,4.6302175937685215,2.645837625018144,
    1.5875031750063502];
  var crs=new L.Proj.CRS('EPSG:3978',
    '+proj=lcc +lat_1=49 +lat_2=77 +lat_0=49 +lon_0=-95 +x_0=0 +y_0=0 +datum=NAD83 +units=m +no_defs',
    {origin:[-34655800,39310000],resolutions:res,
     bounds:L.bounds([-4926564.35,-5396818.74],[5347435.65,4623181.26])});
  var map=L.map('elevmap',{crs:crs,minZoom:2,maxZoom:12,zoomSnap:0.25,attributionControl:true});
  L.tileLayer('https://maps-cartes.services.geo.ca/server2_serveur2/rest/services/BaseMaps/CBME_CBCE_HS_RO_3978/MapServer/tile/{z}/{y}/{x}',
    {minZoom:2,maxZoom:13,attribution:'Hillshade © NRCan CBME (OGL)'}).addTo(map);
  // Frame Canada robustly in Lambert: a 2-corner fitBounds misses the SE/NW bulges of a conic
  // projection, so project the geographic extremes to pixels, take that box, and fit it.
  (function(){var Z=4,
    ex=[[41.7,-141],[41.7,-52.6],[83.1,-141],[83.1,-74],[47,-52.6],[49,-123.5],[60,-141],[52,-56]],
    ps=ex.map(function(a){return crs.latLngToPoint(L.latLng(a[0],a[1]),Z);}),
    xs=ps.map(function(p){return p.x;}), ys=ps.map(function(p){return p.y;}),
    nw=crs.pointToLatLng(L.point(Math.min.apply(null,xs),Math.min.apply(null,ys)),Z),
    se=crs.pointToLatLng(L.point(Math.max.apply(null,xs),Math.max.apply(null,ys)),Z);
    map.fitBounds(L.latLngBounds(nw,se),{padding:[20,16]});})();

  var provLayer=L.geoJSON(E.prov,{style:{color:'#9aa0a6',weight:0.8,fill:false,opacity:0.65}});
  var hydro=L.layerGroup();
  L.geoJSON(E.lakes,{style:{color:'#6699bb',weight:0.4,fillColor:'#a7cde4',fillOpacity:0.75}}).addTo(hydro);
  L.geoJSON(E.rivers,{style:{color:'#6699bb',weight:1.1,opacity:0.85}}).addTo(hydro);
  var cityLayer=L.layerGroup();
  E.cities.forEach(function(c){
    L.circleMarker([c.lat,c.lon],{radius:3,color:'#111',weight:1,fillColor:'#fff',fillOpacity:1}).addTo(cityLayer);
    L.marker([c.lat,c.lon],{icon:L.divIcon({className:'city-label',html:c.name,iconSize:[80,14],iconAnchor:[-5,7]}),
      interactive:false,keyboard:false}).addTo(cityLayer);
  });
  // peaks: coloured-by-elevation dots (hover names); highest-per-province labels appear when zoomed in
  var peakLayer=L.layerGroup(), peakLabels=L.layerGroup(), seen={};
  E.peaks.features.forEach(function(f){
    var c=f.geometry.coordinates,p=f.properties,h=p.elevation_m||0;
    var r=2+Math.max(0,(h-800))/1100;
    L.circleMarker([c[1],c[0]],{radius:r,color:'#3a2a12',weight:0.5,fillColor:elevColor(h),fillOpacity:0.9})
      .bindTooltip(p.name+' — '+Math.round(h).toLocaleString()+' m',{direction:'top'}).addTo(peakLayer);
    if(!seen[p.province]){seen[p.province]=true;
      L.marker([c[1],c[0]],{icon:L.divIcon({className:'peak-label',html:p.name,iconSize:[90,14],iconAnchor:[-4,14]}),
        interactive:false,keyboard:false}).addTo(peakLabels);}
  });
  // ecozones (categorical fills in a pane below the markers) + Arctic tree line — off by default
  map.createPane('ecoPane'); map.getPane('ecoPane').style.zIndex=250;
  var EZCOLORS={"Arctic Cordillera":"#c9d6df","Northern Arctic":"#dde6ec","Southern Arctic":"#b7c9b0",
    "Taiga Plains":"#9bc4a6","Taiga Shield":"#74b08e","Taiga Cordillera":"#a7c8ad","Hudson Plains":"#6db6b3",
    "Boreal Shield":"#2e7d52","Boreal Plains":"#7fb069","Boreal Cordillera":"#5fa07a","Pacific Maritime":"#2f8f8f",
    "Montane Cordillera":"#8a9a5b","Atlantic Maritime":"#4e9c7d","Mixedwood Plains":"#a7d06e","Prairies":"#e3c469"};
  var ecoLayer=L.geoJSON(E.ecozones,{pane:'ecoPane',
    style:function(f){return {fillColor:EZCOLORS[f.properties.ecozone]||'#cccccc',color:'#777',weight:0.3,fillOpacity:0.34};},
    onEachFeature:function(f,l){l.bindTooltip(f.properties.ecozone,{sticky:true});}});
  var treeLayer=L.geoJSON(E.treeline,{style:{color:'#1f6b3a',weight:2.5,opacity:0.95,dashArray:'5,4'}})
    .bindTooltip('Arctic tree line (taiga–tundra boundary)',{sticky:true});
  // permafrost zones (categorical, same low pane; off by default)
  var PFCOLORS={"Continuous (90–100%)":"#08519c","Extensive discontinuous (50–90%)":"#3182bd",
    "Sporadic discontinuous (10–50%)":"#6baed6","Isolated patches (0–10%)":"#bdd7e7"};
  var pfLayer=L.geoJSON(E.permafrost,{pane:'ecoPane',
    style:function(f){return {fillColor:PFCOLORS[f.properties.zone]||'#ccc',color:'#5a7a9a',weight:0.3,fillOpacity:0.5};},
    onEachFeature:function(f,l){l.bindTooltip('Permafrost: '+f.properties.zone,{sticky:true});}});
  // categorical legends shown only while their layer is on
  function catLegend(title,colorMap,order,cols){
    var c=L.control({position:'topleft'});
    c.onAdd=function(){var d=L.DomUtil.create('div','elev-legend cat-legend');
      d.innerHTML='<b>'+title+'</b><div style="column-count:'+(cols||1)+'">'+order.map(function(k){
        return '<div><i style="background:'+colorMap[k]+'"></i>'+k+'</div>';}).join('')+'</div>';
      return c._d=d;};
    return c;
  }
  var ecoLegend=catLegend('Ecozones',EZCOLORS,["Arctic Cordillera","Northern Arctic","Southern Arctic",
    "Taiga Cordillera","Taiga Plains","Taiga Shield","Hudson Plains","Boreal Cordillera","Boreal Plains",
    "Boreal Shield","Montane Cordillera","Pacific Maritime","Prairies","Mixedwood Plains","Atlantic Maritime"],2);
  var pfLegend=catLegend('Permafrost (~1995)',PFCOLORS,["Continuous (90–100%)",
    "Extensive discontinuous (50–90%)","Sporadic discontinuous (10–50%)","Isolated patches (0–10%)"],1);
  // ecozones & permafrost are both area fills → mutually exclusive. Toggling the other off is
  // deferred to after Leaflet finishes handling the current click (its control ignores layer
  // changes while mid-click), and routed through the checkbox so box + legend stay in sync.
  function uncheckLayer(name){document.querySelectorAll('#elevmap .leaflet-control-layers-overlays label').forEach(function(l){if(l.textContent.trim()===name){var i=l.querySelector('input'); if(i&&i.checked)i.click();}});}
  map.on('overlayadd',function(e){
    if(e.layer===ecoLayer){ecoLegend.addTo(map); if(map.hasLayer(pfLayer))setTimeout(function(){uncheckLayer('Permafrost');},0);}
    if(e.layer===pfLayer){pfLegend.addTo(map); if(map.hasLayer(ecoLayer))setTimeout(function(){uncheckLayer('Ecozones');},0);}
  });
  map.on('overlayremove',function(e){if(e.layer===ecoLayer)map.removeControl(ecoLegend);if(e.layer===pfLayer)map.removeControl(pfLegend);});
  provLayer.addTo(map); hydro.addTo(map); cityLayer.addTo(map); peakLayer.addTo(map);
  function syncLabels(){var z=map.getZoom();
    if(z>=5){if(!map.hasLayer(peakLabels))peakLabels.addTo(map);}else{if(map.hasLayer(peakLabels))map.removeLayer(peakLabels);}}
  map.on('zoomend',syncLabels); syncLabels();
  L.control.layers(null,{'Peaks':peakLayer,'Cities':cityLayer,'Lakes &amp; rivers':hydro,
    'Province borders':provLayer,'Ecozones':ecoLayer,'Permafrost':pfLayer,'Tree line':treeLayer},
    {collapsed:false,position:'topright'}).addTo(map);
  var lg=L.control({position:'bottomleft'});
  lg.onAdd=function(){var d=L.DomUtil.create('div','elev-legend');
    d.innerHTML='<b>Peak elevation</b><br>'+[['#1a9850','0'],['#a6d96a','1,000'],['#fee08b','2,000'],
      ['#fdae61','3,000'],['#d73027','4,000'],['#7a0177','5,000+ m']].map(function(s){
        return '<i style="background:'+s[0]+'"></i>'+s[1];}).join('<br>');
    return d;};
  lg.addTo(map);
})();
</script>
```

:::

## How high is Canada?

For all its mountains, Canada is a remarkably **low-lying** country. About **three-quarters of its
land sits below 500 metres**, and barely **half a percent rises above 2,000 metres** — the high peaks
of the western Cordillera and the Arctic make up a tiny share of the total area. The chart shows how
the country's land is distributed across elevation bands.

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(dist_csv):
    d = pd.read_csv(dist_csv)
    fig = go.Figure(go.Bar(
        y=d["band"], x=d["pct"], orientation="h", marker_color="#8c6d31",
        text=[f"{p:.0f}%" for p in d["pct"]], textposition="auto",
        hovertemplate="%{y}: %{x:.1f}% of land area<extra></extra>"))
    fig.update_layout(
        plot_bgcolor="white", height=380, margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=10, b=44),
        xaxis=dict(title="Share of Canada's land area", ticksuffix="%", gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        yaxis=dict(autorange="reversed"))
    fig.add_annotation(text="Source: Natural Resources Canada — Medium-Resolution Digital Elevation Model (MRDEM-30, CanElevation), coarse national read. Land area within province/territory boundaries.",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, xanchor="left", y=-0.18, showarrow=False,
        font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
else:
    print("Elevation data not yet available — run `python -m pipeline.build_geography`.")
```

## Download data

- [Named peaks of Canada (CSV)](../data/geo/peaks_canada.csv) — Canadian Geographical Names + MRDEM-30 elevation
- [Named peaks of Canada (GeoJSON)](../data/geo/peaks_canada.geojson)
- [Land area by elevation band (CSV)](../data/geo/elevation_distribution.csv) — NRCan MRDEM-30
